About Me

Let me introduce myself.

I’m a B.S. Information Science student at CU Boulder with a UX Design Certificate, driven by a simple belief: technology should be as intuitive as it is powerful. My work sits at the crossroads of AI integration, data visualization, and human-centered design — whether that means cutting research time in half with a well-crafted AI workflow, or building interactive platforms that make complex data approachable for real people.

Before CU Boulder I attended Hawaii Preparatory Academy on the Big Island, where living in one of the world’s most biodiverse environments gave me a lasting commitment to environmental stewardship. That same care — for the natural world and for the people in it — shapes every project I take on, whether I’m optimizing a business workflow, leading a beach cleanup, or carving switchbacks on a Colorado trail.

Technical Skills

A blend of technical depth and people-first thinking across AI, development, and design tools.

Portfolio

Selected Projects

A sampling of what I’ve built — from ML fairness research to community-driven mapping platforms.

Machine Learning

ML Fairness in Insurance Pricing

Analyzed 1,338 insurance records using Python, identifying an 11% gender pricing disparity driven by smoking-rate confounders. Implemented three fairness algorithms with standardized metrics and built clear visualizations to communicate findings to non-technical stakeholders.

Pythonscikit-learnpandasBias Mitigation
Full-Stack

Interactive Mapping Platform

Community-driven web application with an interactive map interface built with a Python API backend and React.js frontend. Conducted usability testing with 15 participants, iterating on UX design to ensure intuitive navigation and high engagement.

React.jsPython APIUX Research
Data Visualization

AI Usage by Major — Tableau Study

Survey-based research exploring AI adoption patterns across academic disciplines at CU Boulder. Designed the survey instrument, conducted qualitative interviews, and produced an interactive Tableau dashboard revealing significant variation in AI usage across majors.

TableauSurvey DesignQualitative Research
Web Development

University Website Overhaul

Led a full redesign of the CU Information Science department website, improving content findability through enhanced information architecture, responsive layouts, and user-centered visual design. Holds ongoing ownership of all website edits and updates.

HTML / CSSUX DesignInfo Architecture
